113444.explanation: For important themes, ESA sees this as an appropriate tool. For example, we recently used the 'Irsels consultation' to involve residents in an important topic.

113447.explanation: Do, unless. ESA supports combining functions. We find 'always allow' too premature; every situation should be weighed in terms of opportunities and possibilities.

113448.explanation: ESA promotes a healthy and future-proof living environment.

113449.explanation: ESA strongly encourages local entrepreneurship in the municipality. We do not want large logistics centers in the municipality. These are usually owned by international companies and offer little employment and involvement with our municipality.

113450.explanation: Municipality of Eersel will contribute to the legal duty imposed by the national government. If this means that a reception center must be established, we will cooperate.

113451.explanation: ESA supports appropriate housing for every resident in the municipality of Eersel, including labor migrants. We see a good example in a labor migrants’ hotel, like in Hapert.

113453.explanation: ESA does not yet see a necessity to impose a ban here.

113455.explanation: ESA values connection during festive moments. Now that a national fireworks ban has been introduced, we think it is important that the municipality responds to this.

113459.explanation: ESA wants to accelerate the housing production in the municipality. When farmers develop a suitable plan for housing on their land, we support this.

113463.explanation: ESA supports the musical and cultural development of children.

113466.explanation: The current staffing for safety is adequate. ESA is investing in an eco-friendly enforcement officer, aiming to strengthen safety and enforcement in nature areas.

113469.explanation: ESA believes that local residents should have a better chance in the housing market. We support a starter loan for local residents. Additionally, ESA wants a priority scheme for local residents for new-build homes.

113472.explanation: With clear agreements with the owner about management and livability of the holiday home and/or the park.

113473.explanation: ESA wants to accelerate housing production in the municipality. On agricultural land, inside or outside village boundaries, there are opportunities to contribute to this. We must ensure that green neighborhoods arise, connected to the village.

113474.explanation: ESA believes we should offer possibilities for this. There are situations where people are too vulnerable to be obligated to do so.

113476.explanation: ESA believes the municipality must care for all its residents. We try to help people in poverty as much as possible where feasible.

113478.explanation: For this we have established a 'Loading Vision and Placement Policy.' The development of charging plazas and fast chargers is up to commercial parties.

113479.explanation: ESA finds this necessary in residential neighborhoods. On through roads and access roads in the village, this does not have to apply.

113480.explanation: ESA believes the municipality should provide care appropriate and tailored to the personal situation of residents.

113482.explanation: The property tax (OZB) in the municipality of Eersel is lower than in other Kempen municipalities. Nationally, the rate is also low. We believe the OZB may be adjusted annually according to the national inflation rate (indexed).

113487.explanation: We already do this. The municipality must perform its legal task. To do so, there is a priority scheme for status holders for new rental homes. ESA believes there should also be a priority scheme for residents of the municipality of Eersel.

113489.explanation: ESA believes we should limit solar panels on agricultural land, but not ban them completely. We highly value preserving a rural environment. The municipality should also contribute to a sustainable society, partly through the generation of renewable energy.

113492.explanation: ESA has recently invested in a new swimming pool in Eersel. We believe there should always be an opportunity to take swimming lessons and for recreation within the municipality. Parents are primarily responsible for swimming lessons. Financial support is available for low-income families.

113493.explanation: This should not be an obligation at or for primary schools.

113499.explanation: At least car-reduced. ESA would like to investigate this possibility. It is important to keep the accessibility of the shops possible.

113500.explanation: This is not a municipal task.

113501.explanation: ESA considers it very important to help residents with financial problems as early as possible. Therefore, we have enabled these consultation hours and want to continue doing so.

113444.explanation: Residents must be able to participate in decisions on important topics through good participation, such as advisory referenda.

113447.explanation: Often possible, but not automatically. Livability, nature, and traffic must also be considered.

113448.explanation: We support energy cooperatives and resident initiatives for affordable, clean energy.

113449.explanation: They take up a lot of space, cause traffic nuisance, and provide few local jobs.

113450.explanation: Eersel takes responsibility, preferably through small-scale shelter.

113451.explanation: Employers must arrange decent housing, not the community. However, this can also be done in other locations.

113453.explanation: Wood burning causes unhealthy air and nuisance for residents with health consequences.

113455.explanation: We are open to alternatives, but safety, environment, and animal welfare are paramount.

113459.explanation: Housing development must be careful and fit with the village and landscape.

113463.explanation: Cultural education increases opportunities and self-confidence.

113466.explanation: Prevention and social policy are just as important as enforcement.

113469.explanation: This way, young people and starters get more chances for housing.

113472.explanation: This is not a sustainable solution to the housing shortage.

113473.explanation: First, build smartly and appropriately within villages considering environment and nature.

113474.explanation: A social assistance benefit is in most cases a necessity.

113476.explanation: Due to rising costs, more people are getting into financial difficulties. Therefore, the poverty threshold must be adjusted.

113478.explanation: Charging stations are an essential part of the energy transition. They contribute to cleaner transport.

113479.explanation: That makes streets safer and the living environment more pleasant. It is also better for the environment.

113480.explanation: A good relationship between care needs and care provider contributes to the quality of care. Therefore, there should be as much freedom as possible in choosing care providers.

113482.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is a very important source of income for the municipality. The municipality must be able to continue financing facilities. Everyone must contribute according to their ability.

113487.explanation: Status holders have the same housing needs as any other home seeker. Everyone must have equal opportunities in that regard.

113489.explanation: In the context of the energy transition, solar panels are urgently needed. Solar panels on roofs are preferred, but there are various ways to integrate solar panels into the landscape so that they are not perceived as disturbing.

113492.explanation: Every child in the Netherlands should be able to swim. Children without a swimming diploma are often from families with limited financial means.

113493.explanation: Great if it fits within good education.

113499.explanation: More cars mean more nuisance, more danger, and greater environmental impact. Cars also do not fit the historic village image of the Market. That is outdated. We see this as an option for the other population centers as well.

113500.explanation: Isolation is a major problem for people on social assistance. This is partly because they cannot afford a car. Free public transport helps people on social assistance to break out of that isolation.

113501.explanation: Money worries are usually caused by various problems people face and cannot resolve on their own. Early help and guidance can break people out of such a vicious circle.

113444.explanation: Municipal elections are the best referendums: ordinary Eersel residents, elected by their fellow citizens, build knowledge and expertise and thus make nuanced choices. Referendums, on the other hand, are susceptible to populism and reinforce polarization.

113447.explanation: The municipality must go to great lengths to ensure that farmers can earn a good living or make a sustainable transition or business closure. However, the municipality is bound by provincial regulations.

113448.explanation: Support is then focused solely on the process, such as setting up a cooperative and the permit trajectory. No subsidies are possible for the plan itself: projects must be independently profitable.

113449.explanation: Eersel is not a suitable location for distribution centers: there are no railways or waterways, they hardly create employment, and mainly rely on migrant workers. Moreover, the Kempen region does not lack jobs.

113450.explanation: Large reception centers often cause nuisance and make it difficult to truly participate in society. The CDA prefers to limit the inflow and help victims of conflicts in their own region. Those already here, we want to quickly integrate into our villages and neighborhoods.

113451.explanation: Employers are responsible for decent housing, but through an independent third party. When work and housing are controlled by the employer, an unhealthy dependency relationship for employees arises — something that has led to serious abuses in the past.

113453.explanation: Let us, as residents, take each other into account as much as possible. If you burn dry wood well, much nuisance can be prevented.

113455.explanation: CDA Eersel would have preferred that each municipality could decide on fireworks themselves. But due to battles in big cities, it will be quiet at midnight in Eersel. Therefore, we are looking at a new model: fireworks shows organized by trained people from our own community, supported by the municipality.

113459.explanation: If building a house helps farmers with business closure or dismantling barns, we as a municipality should dare to relax regulations. Of course, the scale and size of the housing must fit the area.

113463.explanation: We must ensure that children from families with tight budgets can also participate. The municipality already subsidizes the music school so lessons are affordable for everyone. But subsidizing all children is a step too far. Support should therefore only be aimed at those who really need it.

113466.explanation: To keep our rural area safe and clean, we need more hands and eyes than we have now.

113469.explanation: We look carefully at all possibilities to help first-time homebuyers. This means first and foremost building! And ensuring that the supply in terms of price and size matches the demand. After that, we must remove remaining obstacles for young people.

113472.explanation: This can be allowed on an incidental basis. But it should not be large-scale generic policy.

113473.explanation: Caregiver homes, space-for-space (building a house in exchange for dismantling a barn), and splitting large homes can help alleviate the housing shortage and increase livability in the rural area.

113474.explanation: There is already an obligation to (volunteer) work with benefits in the Netherlands. This scheme does take certain personal circumstances into account, and that is good.

113476.explanation: The municipality may not supplement incomes but can provide extra support via special assistance and minimum schemes. The threshold for this has already been raised to 120% of the social assistance standard. Further support should be organized by having a village supporter assess on a case-by-case basis what is needed.

113478.explanation: The municipality of Eersel currently has no problem with the availability of charging stations. This is partly because most of our residents have their own driveway.

113479.explanation: There are several roads that serve as main thoroughfares and have separated bike paths. Here, 50 km/h is still appropriate. In other places, the speed can standardly be reduced to 30 km/h.

113480.explanation: People may always choose this, provided it is self-organized and self-funded. But if you are in need of help and dependent on regular institutions, our care is understanding of every religious conviction.

113482.explanation: Property tax (OZB) in Eersel is below the national and Brabant averages. Ultimately, we want good facilities for our residents. But the OZB should never become a cash cow to fill the budget. There must be a direct link to the level of services.

113487.explanation: This would be at the expense of our own residents.

113489.explanation: At peak times, we generate more electricity than too little. The capacity of our electricity network is the real problem we face. So currently, large-scale onshore solar parks are not of great added value.

113492.explanation: Every child must be able to participate; inequality in water safety is unacceptable. Note: schools decide themselves about school swimming. And that is practically difficult for them to implement. The municipality should mainly lower barriers for regular swimming lessons through good facilities and affordability.

113493.explanation: The Brabant dialect is disappearing more and more, and that is a shame. You can only lose it once. Important to emphasize: nothing prevents schools from paying attention to this, and municipalities do not control the content of teaching materials. However, the municipality should look at how it can support schools.

113499.explanation: This would not be practical for the elderly or people from rural areas who come shopping. However, we could look at making the use of the center as a through road somewhat less attractive.

113500.explanation: The province is responsible for public transport, not the municipality. So if you want to make it free, it should be arranged at the provincial level. Such a scheme is very hard for a municipality to organize.

113501.explanation: Weekly assistance in every community center may be a bit exaggerated. The municipality must provide accessible and anonymous help with money worries. A village supporter could assist with this. Help can also take digital or less frequent forms.

113444.explanation: No. Resident input is very important and must be done well and carefully. We will monitor this.

113448.explanation: We welcome such an initiative, and the municipality can already support this. Financial support, in our view, should not come from the municipality (other than existing subsidy schemes).

113449.explanation: There must be space for our Kempen entrepreneurs where our residents can work and who contribute to our society. Distribution centers do not contribute to this.

113450.explanation: In our view, inflow must be reduced. Where necessary, we will comply with the distribution law, but establishment is not a goal in itself.

113451.explanation: We do not find housing seasonal workers in the village centers desirable. They should therefore have housing of sufficient quality at the company or on a campus outside the villages.

113453.explanation: The Eersel VVD is not for a ban, but awareness and education about the wood used and the way of burning are important to us for clean air.

113455.explanation: The fireworks ban is to solve urban problems. This does not occur in our villages. Nevertheless, we must also adhere to this ban. The municipality must therefore handle granting permits well to associations and foundations that still want to set off fireworks.

113459.explanation: We see opportunities to give farmers more freedom while simultaneously addressing the housing shortage. Farmers who want to stop farming or (partially) transition must be able to build homes on their own land under certain conditions. Municipalities retain control to prevent the landscape from becoming cluttered.

113463.explanation: We consider musical education important. The municipality should support it where possible, but the question remains whether this support should be financial.

113466.explanation: Safety is important for the village atmosphere. Because we have a lot of greenery, there must also be enough enforcement officers (BOAs) for our rural area.

113469.explanation: At the national level, the VVD is looking into possibilities for a starter fund to give first-time buyers more borrowing capacity. It is therefore the municipality's task to build the right houses. We will oversee this.

113472.explanation: In this time of housing shortage, we need to be more flexible with rules, but holiday parks must remain holiday parks. We need to build enough houses.

113473.explanation: We have no more space within the borders, so we must build outside them. However, we are in favor of preserving green spaces and do not want the villages to merge into one another.

113474.explanation: That helps people stay engaged in society, making it easier to transition to paid employment.

113476.explanation: Income policy is not the responsibility of the municipality. Nationally, we must ensure that people do not live in poverty and that there are sufficient incentives to work (or continue working).

113478.explanation: There are currently enough charging points.

113479.explanation: At locations where it is logical. This must not come at the expense of, for example, the accessibility for our residents by emergency services.

113480.explanation: This is not the responsibility of the municipality.

113482.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is already low. The VVD in Eersel ensures this remains the case. We oppose any increase if it is not necessary.

113487.explanation: Everyone looking for a home should have a chance to rent here, especially those with ties to the area.

113489.explanation: We believe banning is unnecessary. We do prefer that solar panels be installed on roofs as much as possible.

113492.explanation: This is not the school's responsibility. The safety of our children is our top priority, so initiatives that contribute to this should, in our view, be supported by the municipality.

113493.explanation: Schools decide for themselves about the curriculum, but we find preserving our identity very important. This includes events like carnival parades and the Sinterklaas arrival.

113499.explanation: The layout of the village requires traffic flow through the center.

113500.explanation: Our public transport is too limited for this.

113501.explanation: Reaching people with financial worries is very difficult due to shame. The strength of our villages lies precisely in our large social networks. In our view, these should be used to support those residents with financial concerns whom we have not yet reached.

113444.explanation: Residents must be able to make their voices heard on important issues via a referendum to strengthen participation and engagement.

113447.explanation: We believe farmers should have the opportunity to open a shop or campsite at their business, but this must comply with municipal rules and contribute to the well-being of the surroundings.

113448.explanation: We support sustainable energy and initiatives from residents but believe these should not be structurally dependent on municipal subsidies.

113449.explanation: We want to provide space for economic development and employment, preferably with a diverse economy. New distribution centers should not be blocked outright but carefully assessed in terms of spatial integration, livability, and sustainability.

113450.explanation: Whether an asylum seekers’ center (AZC) is established in our municipality is decided by the national government via the distribution law, so outside our control. However, municipalities can make policies regarding livability, infrastructure, and integration, and consider how reception fits local circumstances.

113451.explanation: Safety and good living conditions for seasonal workers are paramount.

113453.explanation: We believe open fireplaces and wood stoves may remain allowed in Eersel, but within rules that protect health, air quality, and safety, so that enjoying warmth and atmosphere remains responsible.

113455.explanation: We disagree with a municipal fireworks show on New Year’s Eve.

113459.explanation: We disagree that farmers should be allowed to build houses independently on their land. Spatial planning, safety, and sustainability require that building plans always comply with municipal rules and be approved by the municipality.

113463.explanation: Every primary school child should have the opportunity to take music lessons. Municipal subsidies can make this possible for everyone, ensuring talent development, creativity, and enjoyment of music are accessible to all children.

113466.explanation: Safety is a joint responsibility. All stakeholders must collaborate on a shared vision and enforcement of rules. This may lead to more enforcement officers (BOAs), but the focus is mainly on cooperation, prevention, and enforcement within the community.

113469.explanation: Starters can be helped with advice and information on schemes, but financial support remains primarily the responsibility of the market and national programs.

113472.explanation: Housing policy and livability require that homes fit the designated use, so that pressure on the housing market and living environment remains manageable. Permanent residence in a holiday home is not appropriate.

113473.explanation: New homes are important for livable villages and a healthy housing market. There is also an urgent need for sufficient homes in Eersel. Naturally, they must be carefully integrated into the landscape, with suitable infrastructure and attention to sustainability.

113474.explanation: We disagree that people receiving social assistance should be obliged to do community work. We do think it is important that there are opportunities for volunteering and participation, but this must be voluntary and appropriate to each person’s situation.

113476.explanation: We disagree that the income threshold to receive poverty assistance should be raised. More important is that existing schemes are well known and that tailored help is provided to those who really need it.

113478.explanation: Whether more parking spaces are converted to charging points for electric cars depends on need and location. We find it important that charging infrastructure is available where needed, with smart and efficient use of space and resources.

113479.explanation: We believe the maximum speed within built-up areas should be reduced to 30 km/h. This increases traffic safety, protects vulnerable road users like pedestrians and cyclists, reduces noise pollution, and contributes to a livable and calm residential environment.

113480.explanation: Unless not possible due to capacity and availability. After all, we must also be able to trust that our care providers can work in a culturally sensitive manner.

113482.explanation: We believe property tax (OZB) should be lowered. This allows residents and entrepreneurs to keep more financial space, while the municipality continues to focus on efficient spending and a healthy financial management.

113487.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit should be given priority for social rental housing where possible. Municipalities should carefully coordinate this with the housing market and existing applicants. This is about humane support, not extra privileges.

113489.explanation: We take no position on a ban on solar panels on agricultural land. What matters is the objective being achieved: energy generation, sustainability, or preservation of farmland. Each situation requires a balanced, local assessment.

113492.explanation: We consider swimming skills important, but the municipality does not need to structurally allocate funds for school swimming. There are national funds that support individual children, and the municipality can provide additional information and assistance to help make use of these.

113493.explanation: Dialects are culturally valuable, but it is not primarily the municipality's responsibility.

113499.explanation: We support a low-car city centre. This is desirable to improve livability and safety, but a completely car-free centre could limit economic activity and accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors.

113500.explanation: We disagree that public transport should be free outside peak hours for welfare recipients. However, we do believe mobility should remain accessible, for example through existing schemes or targeted support for those who really need it.

113501.explanation: We agree that there should be a weekly, free, and anonymous walk-in consultation for people with financial worries. This offers low-threshold support, strengthens financial self-reliance, and helps residents detect and address problems early.

statements.title: Eersel should make public transport outside peak hours free for people receiving social assistance benefits.

titleParts.text: Eersel moet openbaar vervoer buiten de spits gratis maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The residents of Eersel should be able to give their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Eersel moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The use of fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted in Eersel.

titleParts.text: In Eersel moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Eersel should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Brabant dialect.
The center of Eersel should become car-free.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be a weekly, free, and anonymous walk-in consultation at the local community centers for people with financial worries.

titleParts.text: Er moet een wekelijks, gratis, en anoniem inloop

titleParts.glossary: A fixed time when residents can ask questions or drop in.

titleParts.text: in de lokale gemeenschapshuizen komen voor mensen met geldzorgen.
